### 1. `src/UsersController.scala` — Line 20

> **`// TODO: parse body, validate, add user`**

The `POST /api/users` handler is a stub. It immediately returns a `201 Created` response without doing any actual work. Remaining tasks:

| Sub-task | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Parse request body** | Read and decode the JSON payload from the incoming `POST` request |
| **Validate input** | Check that required fields (`name`, `email`, etc.) are present and well-formed |
| **Add user** | Append the new `User` to the in-memory `users` list (or persist it — see below) |

### 2. `docs/README.md` — Line 28 (`## TODO` section)

Four high-level work items are listed:

| # | Work Item | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Add database persistence** | The user store is currently in-memory (`var users = List(...)`); it resets on every restart. Needs a real DB backend. |
| 2 | **Add authentication middleware** | No auth layer exists yet; all endpoints are currently open. |
| 3 | **Add input validation on POST** | Pairs with the code-level TODO — validate `POST /api/users` payloads before accepting them. |
| 4 | **Add pagination for user listing** | `GET /api/users` returns all users in one shot; needs `page`/`limit` query-param support. |
